Strongs 3339, 3444-3446, 4832-4833. θεμέλιος ≈100¬≠foundationthemelios Strongs 2310-2311, 7652. φίλος ≈450¬≠friendphilos Strongs 2705, 4375, 5368-5373, 5377-5378, 5383-5384, 9639. κοράσιον ≈40¬≠girlkorasion Strongs 1359, 2877. δόξα ≈900¬≠glorydoxa Strongs 1391-1392, 1740-1741, 2754-2755, 3861, 4888, 6141, 6960-6961, 7229, 8608. πορεύω ≈1,900¬≠goporeuō Strongs 1279, 1531, 1607, 1975, 3899, 4197-4198, 4207, 4313, 4365, 4848, 7127, 9306. αἴξ ≈100¬≠goataix Strongs 0122, 6167. χάρις ≈700¬≠gràcecharisGrace, forgiveness, thanksgiving, or rejoicing. Root of "charism" and "eucharist." In Greek, these words are closely related, as some of them are in Latin, in Italian, and in Spanish. Here, they are represented by distantly related English words, each of which includes a grave accent ("à" or "è") to remind us of their closer relation in Greek. In "euchàrist," the prefix "eu-" has been transliterated instead of translation. "Chèer," while apparently unrelated in English, represents the related Greek word for joy. Φοίβη 1¬PhoebePhoibē Strongs 5402. Φοινίκη ≈50¬PhoeniciaPhoinikēPhoenicia, Phoenician purple, and a Phoenician palm. Nobody knows which meaning was original. 70% of all occurences in the NT/LXX refer to the palm.
